**Argeș County** (Romanian pronunciation: [[ˈardʒeʃ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:Ro-Arge%C8%99.ogg "File:Ro-Argeș.ogg")) is a county (*[județ](/wiki/Jude%C8%9B "Județ")*) of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), in [Muntenia](/wiki/Muntenia "Muntenia"), with the county seat at [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești").

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arge%C8%99_County&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Demographics")]

At the [2021 census](/wiki/2021_Romanian_census "2021 Romanian census"), the county had a population of 569,932 and the population density was 83.1/km2 (215.1/sq mi). At the [2011 census](/wiki/2011_Romanian_census "2011 Romanian census"), it had a population of 612,431 and the population density was 89.2/km2 (231.2/sq mi).[[3]](#cite_note-INSSER-3)

* [Romanians](/wiki/Romanians "Romanians") – 97%
* [Roma](/wiki/Romani_people_in_Romania "Romani people in Romania") (Gypsies) and [other ethnic groups](/wiki/Minorities_of_Romania "Minorities of Romania") – 3%

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arge%C8%99_County&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e2/Arge%C5%9F_County_Romania_bgiu.jpg/250px-Arge%C5%9F_County_Romania_bgiu.jpg)](/wiki/File:Arge%C5%9F_County_Romania_bgiu.jpg)

Landscape in central and southern Argeș County

This county has a total area of 6,862 km2 (2,649 sq mi). The landforms can be split into 3 distinctive parts. In the north side there are the mountains, from the [Southern Carpathians](/wiki/Southern_Carpathians "Southern Carpathians") group – the [Făgăraș Mountains](/wiki/F%C4%83g%C4%83ra%C8%99_Mountains "Făgăraș Mountains") with [Moldoveanu Peak](/wiki/Moldoveanu_Peak "Moldoveanu Peak") (2,544 m), [Negoiu Peak](/wiki/Negoiu_Peak "Negoiu Peak") (2,535 m) and Vânătoarea lui Buteanu peak (2,508 m) towering the region, and in the North-East part the [Leaotă Mountains](/wiki/Leaot%C4%83_Mountains "Leaotă Mountains"). Between them there is a pass towards [Brașov](/wiki/Bra%C8%99ov "Brașov"), the [Rucăr–Bran Pass](/wiki/Ruc%C4%83r%E2%80%93Bran_Pass "Rucăr–Bran Pass"). The heights decrease, and in the center there are the sub-carpathian hills, with heights around 800 m (2,600 ft), crossed with very deep valleys. In the south there is the northern part of the [Wallachian Plain](/wiki/Wallachian_Plain "Wallachian Plain").

The main river that crosses the county is the [Argeș River](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_River "Argeș River") in which almost all the other rivers coming from the mountains flow. In the south the main rivers are the [Vedea River](/wiki/Vedea_River "Vedea River") and the [Teleorman River](/wiki/Teleorman_River "Teleorman River").

The county is one of the most industrialized counties in [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"). There is one oil refinery and two automobile plants at [Mioveni](/wiki/Mioveni "Mioveni") – the [Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia") Renault car plant, and at [Câmpulung](/wiki/C%C3%A2mpulung "Câmpulung") the [ARO](/wiki/ARO_(company) "ARO (company)") plant.

The predominant industries in the county are:

* Automotive
* Chemical
* Electrical equipment
* Home appliances
* Food
* Textiles
* Construction materials

Oil is being extracted in the center and in the south. Also there are a few coal mines and close to Mioveni there is a nuclear research and production facility making nuclear fuels for the [Cernavodă](/wiki/Cernavod%C4%83 "Cernavodă") [Nuclear Electric Power Plant](/wiki/Nuclear_power_plant "Nuclear power plant"). On the [Argeș River](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_River "Argeș River") there are a great number of hydroelectric power plants, the most impressive being the [Vidraru](/wiki/Vidraru "Vidraru") power plant and dam.

The hillsides are well suited for wines and fruit orchards, and the south is suited for cereal crops.

The county was located in the central-southern part of the [Greater Romania](/wiki/Greater_Romania "Greater Romania"), in the western part of the historic [Muntenia](/wiki/Muntenia "Muntenia") region. Its territory comprised a large part of the current county, and a piece of the western part of the present [Vâlcea County](/wiki/V%C3%A2lcea_County "Vâlcea County"). It was bordered on the west by the counties of [Olt](/wiki/Olt_County "Olt County") and Vâlcea, to the north by the counties [Făgăraș](/wiki/F%C4%83g%C4%83ra%C8%99_County "Făgăraș County") and [Sibiu](/wiki/Sibiu_County "Sibiu County"), to the east by the counties [Muscel](/wiki/Muscel_County "Muscel County") and [Dâmbovița](/wiki/D%C3%A2mbovi%C8%9Ba_County "Dâmbovița County"), and to the south by the counties [Teleorman](/wiki/Teleorman_County "Teleorman County") and [Vlașca](/wiki/Vla%C8%99ca_County "Vlașca County").

### Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arge%C8%99_County&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Administration")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e4/1938_map_of_interwar_county_Arges.jpg)](/wiki/File:1938_map_of_interwar_county_Arges.jpg)

Map of Argeș County as constituted in 1938.

The county was originally (to 1925) divided administratively into five districts (*[plăși](/wiki/Plas%C4%83 "Plasă")*):[[6]](#cite_note-Memoria-6)

1. Plasa Argeș, headquartered at [Curtea de Argeș](/wiki/Curtea_de_Arge%C8%99 "Curtea de Argeș")
2. Plasa Dâmbovnic, headquartered at [Rociu](/wiki/Rociu,_Arge%C8%99 "Rociu, Argeș")
3. Plasa Oltul, headquartered at [Jiblea Veche](/wiki/Jiblea_Veche,_V%C3%A2lcea "Jiblea Veche, Vâlcea")
4. Plasa Teleorman, headquartered at [Costești](/wiki/Coste%C8%99ti,_Romania "Costești, Romania")
5. Plasa Uda, headquartered at [Uda](/wiki/Uda,_Arge%C8%99 "Uda, Argeș")

Subsequently, Plasa Uda was divided into two districts, and some territory was transferred from Plasa Oltul:

5. Plasa Cuca, headquartered at [Cuca](/wiki/Cuca,_Arge%C8%99 "Cuca, Argeș"), which town was formerly in Plasa Oltul
6. Plasa Pitești, headquartered at [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești")

### Population

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arge%C8%99_County&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Population")]

According to the 1930 census data, the county population was 257,378 inhabitants, out of which 97.6% were ethnic Romanians.[[7]](#cite_note-Rec_1930_16-7) From the religious point of view, the population was 99.1% Eastern Orthodox, 0.3% Roman Catholic, 0.3% Jewish, as well as other minorities.

#### Urban population

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arge%C8%99_County&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Urban population")]

In 1930, the county's urban population was 26,341 inhabitants, comprising 90.4% Romanians, 2.2% Jews, 2.0% Hungarians, 1.7% Romanies, 1.1% Germans, as well as other minorities.[[7]](#cite_note-Rec_1930_16-7) From the religious point of view, the urban population was composed of 93.0% Eastern Orthodox, 2.4% Roman Catholic, 2.4% Jewish, 0.7% Reformed, 0.7% Lutheran, as well as other minorities.
